metadata.seed: So work's a little bit of a different story. We have our daily meetings, and. I. Actually had to pull, like, one of my coworkers aside because I feel like they undermine me all the time, and they, like, they don't give me the respect, basically, that I deserve. So I took her outside after the meeting and leaned into her about it. And afterwards, I mean, the next day, because we have these meetings every day, she, like, she came back and came at me again, but it was worse even after talking to her.

patient.query: So work's a little bit of a different story. We have our daily meetings, and. I. Final Response:
It sounds like there's been some tension with your coworker, and it's understandable that you feel disrespected. Can we take a closer look at why you think she undermines you and how that makes you feel?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s been really tough with her. I often feel like she dismisses my ideas or just doesn’t take me seriously. It makes me really frustrated and anxious, like maybe I’m not good enough to be in this role or something. I don’t know, it just feels like she’s always questioning me, and it's exhausting.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess that’s part of it. I just wish I could express my thoughts without feeling like I’m going to be shot down. Sometimes I wonder if it’s just me overreacting, but it really gets to me. It’s like this weight on my shoulders, you know?

patient.query: I feel like I might be overreacting when I get really upset about small comments she makes. Like, just the other day, I brought up an idea in a meeting, and she just brushed it off without even considering it. I know it might seem silly, but it felt personal, like she didn't value my opinion at all. Then I start questioning myself, wondering if I’m just being too sensitive. It’s confusing.

entailment: Tends to exaggerate emotional expression inappropriately (finding)
neutral: Tends to be sensitive to criticism (finding)

Are you finding that you often question your own worth or abilities when faced with criticism, even if it seems minor? It could be helpful to examine whether this thought pattern is serving you well or causing unnecessary distress.

patient.query: I guess I do tend to spiral when I get a bit of criticism, even if it’s small. It’s like this immediate voice in my head that tells me I’m not good enough, and then everything just feels overwhelming. I want to shake it off, but it lingers and affects my confidence. It’s frustrating because I know I can do the job, but those moments make me doubt myself so much.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just hard to shake those feelings off sometimes. I want to be confident in what I do, but then a little pushback can send me into a tailspin. I keep thinking about how I might be perceived, and that just makes everything feel heavier. I wish I could just brush it off like it doesn’t matter, but it does.

patient.query: I guess it does give me a lot of anxiety. I just can’t shake the feeling that if I say the wrong thing, people will think less of me. It’s like this constant pressure to prove myself, and sometimes I just freeze up. I know I shouldn't care so much, but it feels so real in those moments. It’s like I become this shadow of myself, just trying to avoid making any waves.
